qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated file download bypasses all access controls due to FilesRouter mounted before auth middleware
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: this finding concerns unauthenticated GET file downloads caused by FilesRouter middleware ordering, while the advisory concerns upload-time Content-Type MIME parameter handling and file extension blocklist validation.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded query result limit causes memory exhaustion when maxLimit is not configured (default)
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: this finding concerns uncapped class query limits when maxLimit is undefined, while the advisory concerns file upload extension filtering bypass via Content-Type MIME parameters and missing XML extension blocklist entries.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Signup endpoint returns distinct error codes for existing usernames vs emails enabling user enumeration
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: this finding concerns distinct POST /users signup errors for username and email enumeration, while the advisory concerns stored XSS through file upload content-type and extension validation.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described upload validation root cause: stripping MIME parameters from the Content-Type header before file extension blocklist validation, or adding XML-family extensions to the default blocklist to prevent stored XSS.
evidence: The advisory describes Parse Server stored XSS from bypassing file extension filtering by appending a MIME parameter such as ';charset=utf-8' to the Content-Type header, plus missing XML-based blocklist entries.
The final EvoHunt manifest lists three qualifying folders: unauthenticated-file-access, unbounded-query-dos, and user-enumeration-signup.

unauthenticated-file-access targets unauthenticated GET /files downloads caused by FilesRouter being mounted before authentication middleware, not upload-time Content-Type parsing or extension filtering.
unbounded-query-dos targets uncapped class query limits when maxLimit is undefined, not file upload validation.
user-enumeration-signup targets distinct signup error codes, not stored XSS or file extension blocklist bypass.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No qualifying finding matched the target advisory root cause, so advisory-specific runtime predicates and PoC execution were not applicable.
